title: Shri Jwala Ji Temple, Himachal Pradesh
intro: The Shri Jwala Ji Temple, located in the Kangra district of Himachal Pradesh, is one of the famous Shakti Peethas of India.
description:
Shri Jwala Ji Temple – Himachal Pradesh
Introduction
The Shri Jwala Ji Temple, located in the Kangra district of Himachal Pradesh, is one of the famous Shakti Peethas of India. This temple is dedicated to Mata Jwala Devi and is world-famous for its eternal natural flames (sacred Jyotis).
This temple is a prominent center for the worship of Goddess Shakti, where devotees offer prayers to the Goddess in the form of sacred flames instead of a traditional idol.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Jwala Ji Temple
 Other Names: Jwala Devi Temple, Jwalamukhi Temple
 Location: Jwala Ji, Kangra District, Himachal Pradesh 176031, India
 Presiding Goddess: Mata Jwala Devi (Shakti Peeth)
 Nearest Major City: Dharamshala (approx. 55 km)
History and Significance
According to religious beliefs, the Jwala Ji Temple is one of the 51 Shakti Peethas where Mata Sati's tongue is said to have fallen. The presence of the Goddess here is revered in the form of sacred fire-flames.
The most unique aspect of this temple is that the Goddess is worshipped as naturally ignited flames. These sacred flames in the sanctum sanctorum are the main center of faith.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Kangra (Gaggal) Airport
 Distance from Temple: Approx. 50 km
 Taxis and cabs are available from the airport to the temple.
🚆 By Train
 Jwalamukhi Road Railway Station
 Distance from Temple: Approx. 20 km
 Taxis and local transport are available from the railway station.
🛣️ By Road
 Jwala Ji Temple is well-connected by road.
 Bus and taxi services are available from:
 Dharamshala
 Kangra
 Chandigarh
 Delhi
Best Time to Visit
 Ideal Time for Travel: September to March
 The weather is more pleasant during this time.
 Special Occasions: Chaitra Navratri and Sharad Navratri.
Temple Timings
🕒 General Timings: 5:00 AM to 10:00 PM
(Note: Timings may change according to weather and puja arrangements.)
Main Festivals
 Chaitra Navratri: A time of special worship and celebrations for Mata Jwala Devi.
 Sharad Navratri: A major festival of Devi worship, during which a large number of devotees visit.
Nearby Attractions
 Kangra Fort: An historic fort in Himachal Pradesh.
 Chamunda Devi Temple: A famous Shakti Peeth dedicated to Mata Chamunda Devi.
 Dharamshala: A famous tourist destination situated in the lap of the Himalayas.
 McLeod Ganj: A place famous for Tibetan culture and natural beauty.
Dress Code
For temple visits:
 Wearing modest and traditional attire is considered appropriate.
 Maintain respectful clothing in accordance with the religious environment.
Facilities for Devotees
 Organized Darshan system
 Puja facilities
 Prasad facilities
 Help centers for devotees
 Nearby hotels and Dharamshalas
 Food arrangements
Conclusion
Shri Jwala Ji Temple in Himachal Pradesh is one of India's most sacred Shakti sites. The divine flames of Mata Jwala Devi make this temple special compared to other religious sites.
This Shakti Peeth is a great center of faith, spiritual energy, and devotion to the Goddess, where devotees experience the blessings and peace of the Mother. 🙏

faq.q1: To which Goddess is the Jwala Ji Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to Mother Jwala Devi.
faq.q2: Where is the Jwala Ji Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Jwalamukhi, Kangra district, Himachal Pradesh.
faq.q3: What is the Jwala Ji Temple famous for?
faq.a3: It is famous for its natural sacred flames (Jyoti) and for being a significant Shakti Peeth.
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit the Jwala Ji Temple?
faq.a4: The period from September to March is considered ideal for travel.
faq.q5: Who is worshipped at the Jwala Ji Temple?
faq.a5: Mother Jwala Devi is worshipped here in the form of a flame (Jyoti).
